mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack severely constrains consumer spending growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real state and home construction spending combined with stable consumer price inflation signals robust domestic demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates expanding corporate capacity utilization and earnings momentum, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which systematically supports positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annually, corporate earnings expectations deteriorate rapidly, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in greater than 50% of quarters following sub-1.5% GDP growth, invalidating the earnings deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates alongside accelerating consumer price inflation indicate a tightening monetary policy response that compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive returns in greater than 55% of observations, or inflation-adjusted rate changes show no predictive power for equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts during a credit expansion phase signal strong consumer confidence and leveraged demand, which translates into sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns during credit expansions, with directional accuracy dropping below 48%.
